title: Namespaces, Metadata, Labels, Selectors, Ownership, and Deletion · API Object Lens: K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery
summary: Several teams share a cluster where a selector overlaps another release, an owner is recreated, and a finalizer leaves a namespace terminating. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s finalizers deletion recovery increment in resource identity and lifecycle policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret deletionTimestamp and finalizers, identify the responsible controller, preserve external cleanup evidence, and repair stuck deletion safely.
Trace owner references, UIDs, controller ownership, dependents, and foreground, background, or orphan deletion propagation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example namespace contains similarly named objects, copied labels, a dangling owner reference, and a finalizer blocking deletion. Design names, labels, selectors, annotations, ownership, garbage collection, quota boundaries, and deletion evidence. Competency probe: Interpret deletionTimestamp and finalizers, identify the responsible controller, preserve external cleanup evidence, and repair stuck deletion safely. A defensible solution applies K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to resource identity and lifecycle policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-metadata-namespaces-ownership-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-metadata-namespaces-ownership-3
    artifact: metadata-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s metadata namespaces ownership 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      namespace: intake
      labels: app.kubernetes.io/name=status-api
      selector-owner: deployment-status-api
      owner-uid: retained
      deletion: foreground
      finalizer-controller: archive-cleanup
    verify: kubectl get all -n intake --show-labels -o json && kubectl get namespace intake -o json
    failure: Reject namespace-as-sandbox claims, overlapping selectors, name-only owner matching, or blind finalizer removal.
    repair: Separate scope and security, stabilize identity labels, trace owner UIDs and dependents, and identify external cleanup before deletion.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Deleting finalizers immediately is the normal first repair for any terminating resource. This fails the K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
